message:
test(widgets): serialize tracing subscriber tests against shared callsite cache

Seven widget tests (`command_palette`, `input`, `list`, two tests in
`modal/stack`, `table`, `tabs`, `tree`) install per-test
`tracing_subscriber` registries and then exercise widgets whose
internal `tracing::info_span!` / `tracing::event!` callsites have
already been visited by other tests. The global tracing callsite
interest cache is sticky once populated, so a test that installs a
capture subscriber *after* the cache has memoized an earlier
subscriber's interest decision will silently miss the spans and
events it wants to assert against.

Fix by introducing a new crate-internal `tracing_test_support`
module (gated on `#[cfg(all(test, feature = "tracing"))]`) in
`ftui-widgets/src/lib.rs`:

- `TRACE_TEST_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>>` serializes every test that
  installs a subscriber so two tests cannot race to rebuild the
  interest cache.
- `acquire()` locks the mutex, calls
  `tracing::callsite::rebuild_interest_cache()` to clear stale
  per-callsite decisions so the newly installed subscriber is
  actually consulted, and returns a `TraceTestGuard` wrapping the
  held lock.
- `TraceTestGuard::drop` calls `rebuild_interest_cache()` again so
  the *next* test (which may install its own subscriber, or none at
  all) starts from a clean slate.

Every affected test now calls
`let _trace_test_guard = crate::tracing_test_support::acquire();`
immediately before building its `tracing_subscriber::registry()`.
No other test logic changes.

## choreography.rs doc example

The `Choreography::step` doc example still passed `&'static str`
literals like `Action::Emit("filter_changed")` but the `Action`
enum has already been migrated to owned `String`. The example now
uses `.to_string()` for all three `Emit` / `Handle` arguments so
`cargo test --doc` passes and the rendered rustdoc shows a snippet
that actually compiles.
